claim_text: The future of humanity and how we live, work, and play, depends on thoughtful planning and management of the most advanced artificial intelligence systems.
An immediate pause in AI development for at least six months is necessary to allow time to develop necessary safeguards for humanity.
The rapid pace at which advanced AI systems like GPT-4 are developing is alarming and unprecedented.
Consultation with experts in the tech industry who are sounding the alarm about advanced AI technology is needed.
Elon Musk joined other prominent figures in the tech industry in urging the world’s leading artificial intelligence labs to pause the training of new super-powerful systems for six months.
Recent advances in AI present "profound risks to society and humanity".

claim_text: As with any new innovative technology, artificial intelligence can improve people’s lives.
AI can be, and has been, weaponized to inflict harm.
Artificial intelligence uses complex algorithms to generate simulated reality.
This new "deepfake" technology has become more widely accessible in recent years.
Deepfake technology can be used to cheaply and quickly create fake digital content that is so realistic it is hard to tell if it is genuine or not.
The abuse of AI technology can have a massive impact on people’s lives.
Laws must be updated to keep up with this fast emerging technology.
Previous state legislation (AB 602, Berman) passed and signed into law in 2019, created a private right of action for placing someone’s face on pornographic material without their consent.

Assemblyman Tri Ta's proposed AB 1721 would enact a penalty of a fine or up to a year in a county jail for knowingly distributing artificially generated pornography using someone’s likeness without consent.
AB 1721 would extend protections to Californians who may not have the monetary means to file private lawsuits.

claim_text: California consumers have a right to know if foreign governments, such as China, are collecting and misusing their sensitive personal information.
National security experts and consumer protection advocates have raised concerns about foreign governments having sweeping access to sensitive personal information on American citizens.
AI, social media, and information technology possess amazing potential for transforming people's lives for the better.
There is a "sinister aspect" where foreign governments use AI, social media, and information technology tools to harvest sensitive information on American consumers.
AB 364 would place the state of California at the forefront of protecting consumer privacy from foreign government interception.
The bill (AB 364) would apply to AI, information technology, and social media platforms.
AB 364 requires AI, social media, and other technology platforms to disclose to consumers if personal information will be maintained outside of the United States.
AB 364 mandates explicit consent from consumers for a business to maintain personal information outside of the U.S..

The bill prohibits businesses from maintaining healthcare information, financial information, or geolocation data in the custody of a foreign government or a third party owned or controlled by a foreign government.

By passing AB 364, California would not only protect its own consumers but also ignite a much-needed national debate on the important issue of protecting America’s "Data Sovereignty" from foreign threats.

claim_text: As online technology continues to evolve, safeguards must be put in place to protect citizens from exploitation by malicious actors.
Expanding online privacy protection is necessary to create a safer digital environment.
Californians are vulnerable to intrusive privacy violations.
There is an alarming rise in cases of unauthorized content of private citizens being uploaded to the internet.
Once unauthorized content is uploaded, it is extremely difficult to remove.
With artificial intelligence and deepfakes, anyone can become the next victim of image misuse and theft.
Existing laws have allowed the uploading of abuse, leaving victims (like parents of exploited children, women, and men posted non-consensually) to try to get it removed.
AB 392 (referred to as the PROTECT Act) is crucial for strengthening the State’s existing online privacy laws and aims to stop abuse from happening in the first place.

AB 392 would require any individual uploading sexually explicit content to an adult media site to provide a statement, under penalty of perjury, that every individual depicted is at least 18 years of age and has provided consent.

AB 392 would require operators of adult media sites to ensure that sexually explicit content does not include a non-consenting individual or minor and would create a mandatory 48-hour takedown window for non-consensual content.

claim_text: It is critical that children are protected from exploitation in this new digital age.
SB 1381 expands the scope of child pornography laws to include material generated using artificial intelligence.
AI-generated explicit content involving minors needs to be treated with the seriousness it deserves.
As AI technology advances, it is crucial that laws evolve to protect children from new forms of exploitation.
AB 1831 would add depictions of minors created with artificial intelligence to California’s existing child pornography laws.
AB 1831 and SB 1381 aim to ensure that perpetrators of AI-generated explicit content involving minors are held accountable.
These bills (SB 1381 and AB 1831) provide vital protections by addressing the growing threat of AI-generated explicit content involving minors.
